recentLawUpdates.title: World Rugby Law Amendment — Tackle Height Directive
recentLawUpdates.summary: World Rugby Law Amendment — Tackle Height Directive Effective: 1 January 2024 SUMMARY FOR COACHES AND REFEREES World Rugby has implemented a new directive on tackle height across all levels of the game. The directive lowers the legal tackle height and introduces clearer guidance on what constitutes dangerous play. TACKLE HEIGHT The legal tackle height is now defined as at or below the line of the hips.

recentLawUpdates.title: Coaching note — Session debrief 8 June 2024
recentLawUpdates.summary: Coaching note — Session debrief 8 June 2024 Had a great session with the U16s this evening. Focused on the lineout after last week's issues at the maul. Key things I noticed: - The jumpers are not communicating the call clearly enough — the hooker is throwing before the jumper is set - Front lifter keeps coming up too early which is causing the jump to be off-balance - The call needs to come from the number 8, not the 4 or 5 What worked well: the walking lineout drills we tried at the start.
